domains.name: Domain 4: Network Security, Compliance, and Governance

questions.text: A regulated workload requires the organization to connect many VPCs without full mesh peering. Which solution is BEST? connect many VPCs without full mesh peering.
questions.explanation: Transit Gateway is a regional network hub.

questions.text: A regulated workload requires the organization to extend on-prem routing into AWS. Which solution is BEST? extend on-prem routing into AWS.

options.text: Transit Gateway Connect or VPN/DX attachments

questions.explanation: Connect integrates SD-WAN/third-party appliances.

questions.text: The DevOps team is automating releases and must design IPv6-only workloads in VPC. Which design fits best? design IPv6-only workloads in VPC.

options.text: IPv6 CIDR blocks and egress-only IGW

questions.explanation: AWS supports dual-stack and IPv6-only subnets.

questions.text: After a design review, leadership requires the team to segment traffic with centralized inspection. Which approach is MOST reliable? segment traffic with centralized inspection.

options.text: Gateway Load Balancer with firewall appliances

questions.explanation: GWLB inserts transparent appliances in path.

questions.text: During peak traffic, the platform team must allocate IP addresses at scale. What should they do FIRST? allocate IP addresses at scale.

options.text: IPAM in VPC IP Address Manager

questions.explanation: IPAM plans and monitors IP usage.

questions.text: A company needs to private connectivity to SaaS providers. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort? private connectivity to SaaS providers.
questions.explanation: PrivateLink keeps traffic on AWS network.

questions.text: Operations reports a need to multi-account network architecture. What is the MOST operationally efficient solution? multi-account network architecture.

options.text: Shared services VPC via RAM and TGW

questions.explanation: Hub-spoke is common for enterprise networks.

questions.text: After a design review, leadership requires the team to optimize cross-AZ traffic costs. Which approach is MOST reliable? optimize cross-AZ traffic costs.

options.text: Keep chatty tiers in same AZ when possible and use AZ-aware endpoints
Single NAT for all AZ imbalanced

questions.explanation: Cross-AZ traffic has charges; design thoughtfully.

questions.text: During peak traffic, the platform team must encrypt VPN over internet. What should they do FIRST? encrypt VPN over internet.
questions.explanation: VPN uses IPsec tunnels.

options.text: AWS Network Firewall endpoints per AZ

questions.text: The DevOps team is automating releases and must monitor TGW metrics and routes. Which design fits best? monitor TGW metrics and routes.

questions.text: For cost and security, the team must detect network access anomalies. What is the recommended approach? detect network access anomalies.
questions.explanation: GuardDuty analyzes flow and DNS logs.

questions.text: The DevOps team is automating releases and must automate remediation for open SG rules. Which design fits best? automate remediation for open SG rules.
questions.explanation: Automated remediation reduces exposure window.

questions.text: For cost and security, the team must use Network Access Analyzer for unintended access. What is the recommended approach? use Network Access Analyzer for unintended access.
questions.explanation: NAA finds paths that allow unintended access.

questions.text: Which TWO official AWS resources help you prepare for AWS Certified Advanced Networking – Specialty? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: Use official AWS exam guides, Skill Builder, and AWS documentation—not real exam content.

questions.text: Which TWO practices align with the AWS Well-Architected Framework? (Select TWO.)

options.text: Design for failure and automate recovery
Use single-AZ for all production data stores

questions.explanation: Reliability and operational excellence emphasize automation, testing, and learning from failure.

questions.text: Which are AWS services used for encryption or key management? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: KMS manages keys; Secrets Manager stores and rotates secrets.
